retry_reason: Allocation failure (kzalloc) may be transient; retrying after memory becomes available could succeed.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper that returns a batadv_orig_ifinfo with a reference held for the caller. If the entry already exists (found by batadv_orig_ifinfo_get), its refcount is incremented; otherwise, a new entry is allocated, initialized with an initial refcount (kref_init), and added to the origin node's ifinfo_list. The caller must check the return value (NULL on allocation failure) and must release the reference with batadv_orig_ifinfo_put when done. The function also takes a reference on the outgoing interface (if not BATADV_IF_DEFAULT) which is likewise released upon put. The operation is protected by the neigh_list_lock, but the caller does not need to hold any lock.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to return a batadv_orig_ifinfo with an acquired reference (get-or-create). The only non-refcount logic is minimal field initialization and list insertion.
judge.retry_reason: Allocation failure (kzalloc) may be transient; retrying after memory becomes available could succeed.

check.reason: Operation 1 (get on if_outgoing) is valid: kref_get(&if_outgoing->refcount) matches the callee path. Operations 2 and 4 (gets on return) are valid: one from batadv_orig_ifinfo_get (callee get) and one from explicit kref_get(&orig_ifinfo->refcount); both paths and types are consistent. Operation 3 (set on return) is removed because the callee info for kref_init uses path kref.refcount.refs with atomic_t, but the candidate uses path orig_ifinfo.refcount.refcount with type chain ending in refcount_t, which is inconsistent with the actual initialization.
